top_signals.title: Oil falls to pre-Iran war lows as Strait of Hormuz flows normalize
top_signals.summary: Oil prices fell for a fourth straight session on Thursday, sliding to their lowest levels since before the Iran war, as concerns over disruptions to Middle East crude supplies continued to ease amid improving traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. As of 05:55 ET (09:55 GMT), Brent Oil Futures expiring in August fell 1.5% to ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Rates Spark: More repricing risk at front end
top_signals.summary: The front end looks particularly vulnerable to repricing lower A further drop in oil prices is paring inflation risks and is making bonds look more attractive to investors amid fragile risk sentiment. Brent is now trading close to pre-war levels. Markets are nevertheless still positioned for 30bp of ECB tightening, but we think this hawkish ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: NLB Chief Touts 40% Premium, Heritage in Bidding War for Addiko
top_signals.summary: After two years of trying to get a foothold in neighboring Croatia by buying Addiko Bank AG, Slovenia’s largest lender isn’t ready to give up.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Premier League clubs warned crypto deals could be worthless in a year
top_signals.summary: Premier League football clubs have been warned that long-term sponsorship deals with crypto firms could be rendered worthless in a year’s time. It comes after the Financial Conduct Authority took the unusual step of writing to teams about the issue this month and as the watchdog rolls out plans for tighter scrutiny of the sector. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Traders Weigh Interest-Rate Outlook
top_signals.summary: Gold traded in a narrow range, a day after a resurgent US dollar and the prospect of higher interest rates pushed the metal below $4,000 an ounce for the first time since November.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: US crude oil inventories drop to lowest level since 1984
top_signals.summary: U.S. crude oil inventories including the Strategic Petroleum Reserve decreased by more than 15 million barrels during the week ending June 19, reaching 743.3 million barrels, according to data released by the Energy Information Administration on Wednesday. This marks the lowest inventory level since October 1984. Crude oil stocks excluding the SPR fell to the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: SK Hynix surges 12% after Micron earnings; blockbuster Nasdaq listing
top_signals.summary: Shares of South Korean chip giant SK Hynix surged 12% on Thursday after the company said it is seeking to raise as much as $29.4 billion.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Aluminium prices hit three-month lows on weaker risk premium
top_signals.summary: Aluminium prices dropped to their lowest levels in three months on Wednesday as Middle East risk concerns eased and the U.S. dollar strengthened. The London Metal Exchange benchmark three-month aluminium contract fell 0.76% to $3,208 per metric ton by 0701 GMT. Earlier in the session, the contract touched $3,191, marking its weakest point since March ...

top_signals.summary: A senior Islamic State leader was killed by an airstrike last week, Central Command announced on Wednesday.
